frameworks.name: thalamocortical connectivity mapping
frameworks.one_line: Tracer-based comprehensive afferent mapping of cortical inputs to medial pulvinar.

frameworks.name: multimodal integration via cell-cluster microunits
frameworks.one_line: Intermingled projection neurons form clusters supporting cross-modal integration.

frameworks.name: directed attention and associative thalamus
frameworks.one_line: PM cited as multimodal associative nucleus implicated in attention.

core_question: Which cortical areas project to the medial pulvinar in the marmoset, and how are these projections organized?
key_assumptions: MRI-guided tracer injections faithfully reveal cortical afferents
Marmoset PM organization is informative for primate thalamocortical architecture
Reciprocal connectivity is a meaningful organizational principle
manipulations: Tracer injection sites targeted to PM via MRI guidance
Sampling across temporal, parietal, retrosplenial-cingulate, prefrontal, orbital cortex
key_findings: 62 discrete cortices project to adult marmoset PM
New projections identified beyond ~20 previously reported cortices
PM-cortical connectivity is reciprocal across mapped regions
PM neurons projecting to different cortices are intermingled into multimodal cell clusters
interpretations: PM exhibits a microunit organization distinct from the large functional subdivisions typical of thalamic nuclei
Intermingled clusters likely facilitate cross-modal integration
